mnemonics: Leachate And Nutrient Degradation spells LAND - Remember the key threats: Leachate contamination, Acidification, Nutrient depletion, and Degradation of soil quality.
keyPoints: Landfilling is a primary method for e-waste disposal but poses risks of soil and groundwater contamination from heavy metals.
Land degradation from e-waste includes soil acidification and loss of fertility due to the leaching of toxic substances.
Sustainable land management for e-waste involves secure landfill designs, bioremediation techniques, and promoting recycling to reduce landfill burden.
commonMistakes: Assuming all landfills are equally safe; unlike modern secure landfills, open dumps lack liners and leachate collection systems, leading to severe contamination.
Believing that e-waste in a landfill is inert; components can break down over time, releasing persistent toxic substances into the soil and water.
